message:
beads: close ft-26k3h (update_hour NaN guard landed in 44993279)

Flip ft-26k3h (ActivityProfile::update_hour NaN poison) from
in_progress to closed with close_reason "done". The corresponding
code fix and regression tests landed earlier in this sweep at
44993279 (fix(survival): reject NaN in ActivityProfile::update_hour).

Tracker bookkeeping only. No code changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Publish hardware-tier guidance backed by validated unit/integration/e2e/perf evidence from the resize/reflow program.
2. Guidance must include explicit operational playbooks, rollback/fallback steps, and diagnostics workflows for each tier.
3. Require structured logging/diagnostics artifact references so operators can map symptoms to recommended actions quickly.
4. Validate guidance via scripted end-to-end operator journeys and update docs when discrepancies are discovered.
5. Added cross-link from docs/resize-rollout-plan-wa-1u90p.8.2.md so rollout documentation chain now references this guidance.","created_at":"2026-02-20T01:55:29Z"},{"id":2624,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","author":"CobaltCanyon","text":"CobaltCanyon starting a bounded docs/ops slice.  {"id":"ft-26738","title":"restart_scheduler: clamp negative hours_since_last in recency_penalty (LOW)","description":"Fixed in d327d7c1. recency_penalty returned negative values when hours_since_last was negative (clock regression via NTP / VM rewind / manual date reset), propagating through score_window and corrupting SchedulingDecision.windows ranking. Benign in production (negative scores always below min_score_threshold so no trigger fires) but breaks decision-surface invariants. Fix clamps to 0 (treat as just restarted). 2 regression tests pin the invariant. Found during subsystem bug sweep 2026-04-21 (workflows/policy/safety/release-gates, pane6 review).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:27.355801Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:30.892645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:30.892323Z","close_reason":"Fixed in d327d7c1","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-26k3h","title":"[MEDIUM] survival::ActivityProfile::update_hour NaN-poisons hourly_ewma via clamp","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:455` — `ActivityProfile::update_hour`.

Same `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` gotcha as ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce. A NaN `normalized_activity`:

1. `sample = normalized_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` stays NaN.
2. First sample (sample_count == 0) path writes `self.hourly_ewma[index] = NaN`.
3. Any subsequent EWMA update is `alpha * NaN + (1 - alpha) * prev = NaN`.
4. Every `predict_hour` / `predict` call returns NaN for that hour slot, forever.
5. Downstream survival/hazard math using the predicted activity gets NaN — same silent-wrong-answer class as disk_pressure.

## Fix

Guard at entry: if `normalized_activity.is_nan()`, skip the update entirely. The slot keeps its last good value (or the default if uninitialised) and the next well-formed sample continues the EWMA.

## Tests

* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_does_not_poison_ewma` — update_hour(NaN) leaves hourly_ewma + sample_count unchanged.
* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_then_clean_sample_still_tracks` — end-to-end: NaN → clean sample sequence still produces a finite, sensible EWMA value.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:50:37.797018Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:50:51.875839Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-26k3h","title":"[MEDIUM] survival::ActivityProfile::update_hour NaN-poisons hourly_ewma via clamp","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:455` — `ActivityProfile::update_hour`.

Same `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` gotcha as ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce. A NaN `normalized_activity`:

1. `sample = normalized_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` stays NaN.
2. First sample (sample_count == 0) path writes `self.hourly_ewma[index] = NaN`.
3. Any subsequent EWMA update is `alpha * NaN + (1 - alpha) * prev = NaN`.
4. Every `predict_hour` / `predict` call returns NaN for that hour slot, forever.
5. Downstream survival/hazard math using the predicted activity gets NaN — same silent-wrong-answer class as disk_pressure.

## Fix

Guard at entry: if `normalized_activity.is_nan()`, skip the update entirely. The slot keeps its last good value (or the default if uninitialised) and the next well-formed sample continues the EWMA.

## Tests

* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_does_not_poison_ewma` — update_hour(NaN) leaves hourly_ewma + sample_count unchanged.
* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_then_clean_sample_still_tracks` — end-to-end: NaN → clean sample sequence still produces a finite, sensible EWMA value.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:50:37.797018Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:53:29.312035Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:53:29.311735Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2guux","title":"reality-check gaps","description":"Reality-check sweep of the repo (pane 5, session 2026-04-20) against
README.md + AGENTS.md + docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md. Most
capability claims match the code; the four material gaps below are
worth tracking. No fixes this tick — documentation-only finding.

Methodology: read the README "Supported Surface Matrix" and "Quick
Example", then grep the CLI command tree at `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`,
the MCP tools at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs`, the web
endpoints at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/router.rs`, and the
runtime layer at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs` +
`runtime_compat.rs`.

Findings (severity ranked):

G1 [MEDIUM] Watch-daemon asupersync claim vs actual reactor dependency
  Evidence:
    - README.md:38-41 "runtime model is asupersync-native",
      "runtime_compat remains as an audited boundary".
    - crates/frankenterm-core/tests/runtime_labruntime.rs:13-18
      explicit comment:
        "Full ObservationRuntime integration tests (startup,
         shutdown, discovery, capture) require the runtime_compat::task
         module to be migrated from tokio::spawn to asupersync::spawn.
         Until that migration completes, ObservationRuntime::start()
         cannot run under asupersync RuntimeFixture because task::spawn
         → tokio::spawn panics without a tokio reactor."
    - runtime.rs:184 uses `crate::runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx`
      which still goes through the tokio-backed path when the
      `asupersync-runtime` feature is disabled.
  Why it matters: the primary operator surface (`ft watch`) cannot be
  exercised end-to-end under a pure asupersync runtime fixture today.
  The README is honest that runtime_compat is "an audited boundary";
  the specific gap is that the watch daemon's task spawns transit
  that boundary. Tracked by the ft-xbnl0.2.x epic but worth surfacing
  as a doc clarification on README.md:38-41 ("native with a named
  quarantine").

G2 [MEDIUM] `ft restart` is Unix-only, not disclosed in the support matrix
  Evidence:
    - README.md:124 "Session persistence | Supported with backend
      prerequisite | Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and
      `ft session doctor` are implemented".
    - crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27032 emits:
        "ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms."
      and exits 1 on non-Unix; the full `execute_restart_workflow`
      call path sits inside `#[cfg(unix)]` at line 27036.
  Why it matters: a Windows operator following the README's
  session-persistence story will hit this only at runtime.
  Supportable via an asterisk in the matrix or an explicit
  "Unix-only" caveat in the `ft restart` section.

G3 [LOW] Performance claims lack linked artifacts
  Evidence:
    - README.md:51 "<50ms lag" for delta extraction.
    - README.md:62 "keeps 200+ panes under 1GB vs 4GB+".
    - README.md:113 "TOON reduces token consumption by 40-60%".
    - No matching benchmark artifact path in docs/ referenced from
      these claims; the verification-contract doc at docs/ft-xbnl0-
      verification-contract.md mandates Level C artifact bundles for
      soak/perf-adjacent claims but the README does not cite any.
  Why it matters: performance claims presented as current fact in
  the README should either cite an artifact bundle or be softened to
  "target" / "design goal" until evidence lands. Non-blocking.

G4 [LOW] `SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT = 200` silently bounds OSC133 state
  Evidence:
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:188
        `const SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT: usize = 200;`
    - mcp.rs:288-310 `derive_osc_state_from_storage` walks only the
      last 200 segments. If OSC133 markers span further back, the
      derived state is silently incomplete; the helper returns
      `Ok(None)` with no gap marker.
  Why it matters: a quiet-pane edge case (no activity for >200
  segments) would yield an incorrect alt-screen / prompt state via
  the MCP surface without a visible warning. Worth either documenting
  the bound or recording a `markers_unknown_due_to_window_limit`
  signal.

Non-gaps verified present and matching claims:

  - `ft robot state` / `state --include-text`: main.rs:2375-2391
  - `ft robot search --mode {lexical,semantic,hybrid}`:
      main.rs:4633-4711 enum + From impl; semantic/hybrid properly
      gated behind `semantic-search`/`frankensearch` features.
  - `ft mcp serve`: main.rs:33124
  - `ft web` + `/stream/events` + `/stream/deltas`:
      web/router.rs:65,70 — streaming exists; README's
      "more than 'health only'" claim is accurate.
  - `ft distributed agent`: main.rs:165-168 under
      `#[cfg(feature = "distributed")]`.
  - `ft session doctor`, snapshot save/restore/list/inspect/diff:
      SnapshotCommands + SessionCommands enums defined around
      main.rs:4207.
  - `ft tx run`/`tx rollback`/`tx plan`/`tx show`: main.rs:21114+
      via `execute_tx_run_with_executor`.
  - Policy "21 subsystems": unified_telemetry.rs:242 comment; actual
      PolicyEngine struct at policy.rs:3466-3517 declares ~22
      subsystem-shaped fields. Close-enough; no drift worth fixing.
  - tokio/smol usage in supported core: grep finds zero production
      call sites outside `runtime_compat` + `dependency_eradication`
      + `forbidden_dep_guards` (guard files are intentional).

Documented only. No shippable fix this tick per /reality-check-for-project
ground rules. Related: AGENTS.md rule zero (NEVER delete
frankenterm-core) is intact; the four gaps above are additions to the
README, not subtractions from the code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:01:41.726765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.915672Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.913552Z","close_reason":"Reopening-in-error by p5-orch robot-triage pick — the bead was already closed with the four reality-check gaps (G1 runtime_compat quarantine language, G2 ft restart Unix-only caveat, G3 softened perf/token claims, G4 OSC/OSC133 marker window bound) addressed across two commits:

1. 4c3952a9 docs: correct README claims vs actual (ft-2guux) — landed 2026-04-20, covers all four gaps:
   - G1: added 'The remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses a named runtime_compat quarantine while the last task-spawn seams are being collapsed' to the Platform Model paragraph
   - G2: appended 'ft restart is currently Unix-only.' to the Session persistence row of the support matrix
   - G3: softened <50ms lag to 'target/benchmark lane'; softened 200+ pane memory envelope to 'benchmark-dependent until linked artifacts are published'; softened 40-60% TOON savings to 'lower-token machine format; savings vary by payload'
   - G4: added a new subsection '5.1 Marker Window Bound' documenting the bounded OSC/OSC133 marker derivation window

2. af925d26 docs(readme): correct runtime_compat boundary claim — landed 2026-04-21, further refined G1 language now that the 9e1bd63b cfg-strip has collapsed the dual-runtime fallback: 'The dual-runtime fallback is gone, but several production seams still route through the named runtime_compat boundary for runtime bootstrap, channels, time, blocking work, IPC, and mux interoperability while the remaining compat helpers are being collapsed.'

bv --robot-triage surfaced this bead as open-and-ready because the triage graph snapshot had not yet absorbed the prior close. Verified via git log --grep ft-2guux + direct README diff inspection: no work remains. Restoring closed status.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","docs","readme","reality-check"]}
 {"id":"ft-2h5wv","title":"Refactor MuxPool (mux_pool.rs) to asupersync","description":"# Refactor MuxPool (mux_pool.rs) to asupersync

## Background
mux_pool.rs (583 LOC) wraps generic Pool for DirectMuxClient connections with WezTerm-specific health checks.

## Dependencies
- Pool refactoring (wa-36idf) must be done first
- DirectMuxClient refactoring (wa-p48pw) must be done first

## Migration
1. Thread Cx through to Pool methods
2. Update health check (list_panes()) to use Cx
3. Update connection factory (DirectMuxClient::connect) to use Cx
4. Ensure pool config works with Budget model

## Comprehensive unit tests (LabRuntime)
1. **Basic lifecycle**: create pool → acquire → use → release → shutdown
2. **Health check integration**: mock unhealthy client, verify pool replaces it
3. **Connection factory**: verify DirectMuxClient::connect called with correct Cx
4. **Idle eviction**: connections idle past timeout are evicted (virtual time)
5. **Concurrent acquire**: N tasks acquire simultaneously, max_size respected
6. **Acquire timeout**: full pool, Budget::deadline fires, clean Cancelled outcome
7. **Pool drain on shutdown**: scope exit waits for all connections returned
8. **Stats accuracy**: verify all counters under concurrent load

Each test logs: pool stats at each step, health check results, connection create/destroy events.

## Acceptance criteria
- MuxPool works with asupersync DirectMuxClient and Pool
- Health checks work through Cx
- Idle eviction works with virtual time
- 8+ LabRuntime tests
- Obligation leak oracle passes

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Pool concurrency testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to exhaustively test concurrent MuxPool operations:
  - Multiple tasks acquiring connections while health checks run — verify no stale connections returned
  - Interleaved acquire + release + eviction — verify pool invariants (active + idle <= max_size)
  - Concurrent shutdown while tasks hold connections — verify clean drain without orphaned connections
  - Race between health check failure and acquire — verify correct fallback to new connection creation

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for pool scaling with connection count**: Add `benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs` measuring:
  - Acquire + release cycle latency as a function of pool size (max_size=1,2,4,8,16,32)
  - Health check overhead per acquire operation
  - Pool scaling: throughput (ops/sec) with increasing concurrent task count
  - Idle eviction scan time as a function of pool size
  - Connection factory overhead (DirectMuxClient::connect latency)

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Migrate MuxPool async behavior to asupersync while preserving pool correctness, health-check semantics, and acquisition/release guarantees.
2. Validate dependency alignment with DirectMuxClient/pool foundation beads and maintain deterministic behavior under contention and unhealthy-client replacement.
3. Require comprehensive validation: unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end scripts for lifecycle, health-check replacement, backpressure behavior, and timeout/cancellation flows.
4. Require structured diagnostics/logging for pool state transitions (acquire/release/replace/evict), health-check outcomes, and latency metrics.
5. Closure requires rch-offloaded validation evidence and explicit residual-risk notes.","notes":"2026-03-01 AmberDesert progress: added explicit Cx-threaded APIs for pool and mux pool migration boundary.\n\nCode changes:\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/pool.rs: add try_acquire_with_cx/acquire_with_cx, keep existing acquire/try_acquire as wrappers via cx::for_testing().\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs: add list_panes_with_cx/get_lines_with_cx/get_pane_render_changes_with_cx/get_pane_render_changes_batch_with_cx/write_to_pane_with_cx/send_paste_with_cx/health_check_with_cx; migrate internal acquire/recovery flow to *_with_cx path and make legacy methods wrappers.\n- Added focused tests for new Cx surfaces in both files.\n\nValidation:\n- rustfmt --edition 2024 on changed files: PASS.\n- rch exec -- env CARGO_HOME=/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/.cargo-amber2 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-amberdesert2 cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib : PASS (finished dev profile in 2m56s).\n- rch test/check paths for test targets currently unstable due remote worker connectivity failures + long local fallback compiles; timeout hit on bounded test runs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:50:21.992539Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:43:17.346890Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:43:17.346015Z","close_reason":"Ported direct wezterm mux callers onto explicit-Cx pool entry points and made cancellation propagate instead of silently falling back to CLI.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","depends_on_id":"ft-p48pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":429,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SapphireMoose progress slice (2026-02-20): Updated crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs mock servers to handle WriteToPane/SendPaste PDUs and added tests pool_write_to_pane_succeeds + pool_send_paste_reuses_connection. Validation via rch exec (workers unreachable -> local fallback): cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib pool_write_to_pane_succeeds -- --nocapture PASS; cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib pool_send_paste_reuses_connection -- --nocapture PASS. During validation found unrelated unit-test compile blocker in crates/frankenterm-core/src/cx.rs spawn_bounded_empty_tasks (type inference with empty Vec under this config); applied minimal test-only type annotation so vendored lib tests compile.","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:14:36Z"},{"id":430,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Additional validation slice: rch exec (local fallback due worker outage) cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib vendored::mux_pool::tests:: -- --nocapture => 41 passed / 0 failed. Also ran rch exec cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib => PASS (warnings only). Workspace-wide clippy -D warnings remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing issues outside mux_pool scope (rangeset/uds/blob-leases/pty and additional integration-test compile drift).","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:17:26Z"},{"id":431,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Benchmark slice added: crates/frankenterm-core/benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs + Cargo.toml bench registration (required-features=[vendored]). Scenarios implemented: acquire/release cycle latency across max_size {1,2,4,8,16,32}; health_check overhead; throughput scaling across concurrency {1,2,4,8,16,32}; idle eviction scan by pool size; DirectMuxClient::connect overhead. Bench file rustfmt check passed. Targeted bench compile command via rch exec (cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features vendored --bench mux_pool_scaling) blocked by unrelated pre-existing compile errors in crates/frankenterm-core/src/fibonacci_heap.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:20:28Z"},{"id":432,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"TurquoiseGlacier","text":"TurquoiseGlacier slice (2026-02-22): migrated crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs test harness off tokio test/runtime bindings for this module. Changes: (1) replaced all remaining #[tokio::test] in mux_pool.rs with runtime-compat runner style via new local run_async_test helper (RuntimeBuilder::current_thread().build() + block_on), (2) switched fallback unix_stream_read import from tokio::io::AsyncReadExt to crate::runtime_compat::unix::AsyncReadExt, (3) added CompatRuntime trait import required for block_on method resolution. Validation via rch exec: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-turquoise-ft2h5wv CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features vendored --lib vendored::mux_pool::tests:: -- --nocapture => PASS (41 passed, 0 failed). Additional asupersync check attempt via rch exec: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-turquoise-ft2h5wv CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib remains blocked by pre-existing runtime_compat select! gating errors in snapshot_engine/runtime_compat (E0433), but mux_pool.rs no longer emits the prior block_on trait-scope error after this slice.","created_at":"2026-02-22T00:30:50Z"},{"id":433,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"TurquoiseGlacier","text":"Correction/clarification: with current branch HEAD in this shared workspace, the net code delta from my slice is one-line import update in mux_pool tests (use crate::runtime_compat::{CompatRuntime, task, timeout};).
